question.label: Decision bottlenecks
question.name: Decision bottlenecks — where are they stacking up most right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific bottleneck Lin named to locate the main source of current friction.

answer: Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.

assessment.note: She acknowledges work was held too long instead of moving to the team sooner, a mild negative engagement signal around ownership distribution; [THREAD-DEFERRED] delegation thread opened but drill cap forces stage advance.

new_queue.label: Opening pace check
new_queue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?

new_queue.label: Priority clarity
new_queue.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?

new_queue.label: Delegation boundary
new_queue.name: What work are you still holding that someone else could own?

new_queue.label: Steadier rhythm
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel more sustainable?

history.answer_excerpt: The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews.
Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.
The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
